annotations.label: Surjective function

anchor.snippet: A surjective function is a function whose image is equal to its codomain

annotations.note: Function.Surjective f is defined as ∀ b, ∃ a, f a = b, i.e. image equals codomain.

annotations.label: Identity function is surjective

anchor.snippet: the identity function id X on X is surjective

annotations.note: Function.surjective_id states the identity map is surjective.

annotations.label: Parity map onto {0,1}

anchor.snippet: even integers are mapped to 0 and odd integers to 1

annotations.note: The parity map ℤ→{0,1} is the n=2 case of surjectivity of the cast ℤ→ZMod n, but no dedicated parity example exists.

annotations.label: Affine map 2x+1 is surjective

anchor.snippet: because for every real number y

annotations.note: No named lemma formalizes surjectivity of this specific real affine map x↦2x+1.

annotations.label: Cubic x^3-3x is surjective not injective

anchor.snippet: every cubic polynomial with real coefficients has at least one real root

annotations.note: This specific cubic example (surjective but not injective) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square map not surjective on R

anchor.snippet: since there is no real number x such that

annotations.note: The non-surjectivity of x↦x² on ℝ is not stated as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Natural logarithm is surjective

anchor.snippet: The natural logarithm function

annotations.note: Real.log_surjective proves Surjective Real.log.

annotations.label: Matrix exponential surjectivity

anchor.snippet: The matrix exponential is not surjective when seen as a map

annotations.note: Matrix.exp exists but its (non-)surjectivity onto general/special linear groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Projection from a product is surjective

anchor.snippet: The projection from a cartesian product

annotations.note: Prod.fst_surjective (and Prod.snd_surjective) prove the coordinate projections are surjective for nonempty factors.

annotations.label: 3D-to-2D screen projection

anchor.snippet: vectors are projected onto a 2D flat screen

annotations.note: This computer-graphics projection example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bijective iff surjective and injective

anchor.snippet: A function is bijective if and only if it is both surjective and injective

annotations.note: Function.Bijective is defined definitionally as Injective f ∧ Surjective f.

annotations.label: Surjectivity is a property of the mapping

anchor.snippet: surjectivity is not a property of the function itself, but rather a property of the mapping

annotations.note: This is a metamathematical remark about codomain-dependence, not a formal theorem.

annotations.label: Right inverse of a function

anchor.snippet: is said to be a right inverse of the function

annotations.note: Function.RightInverse g f means f ∘ g = id, the right-inverse notion.

annotations.label: Right inverse iff surjection (axiom of choice)

anchor.snippet: Every function with a right inverse is necessarily a surjection

annotations.note: Function.surjective_iff_hasRightInverse proves Surjective f ↔ HasRightInverse f (using choice).

annotations.label: Recovering a subset from its preimage

anchor.snippet: Thus, B can be recovered from its preimage

annotations.note: Set.image_preimage_eq gives f '' (f ⁻¹' s) = s when f is surjective.

annotations.label: Right inverse illustration

anchor.snippet: in the first illustration in the gallery

annotations.note: This is a pictorial illustration, not a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Surjective iff right-cancellative

anchor.snippet: is surjective if and only if it is right-cancellative

annotations.note: injective_comp_right_iff_surjective characterizes surjectivity as right-cancellability of g↦g∘f (over a nontrivial target).

annotations.label: Surjections are epimorphisms in Set

anchor.snippet: surjective functions are precisely the epimorphisms in the category of sets

annotations.note: epi_iff_surjective proves a morphism in Type is an epimorphism iff it is surjective.

annotations.label: Right inverse implies epimorphism

anchor.snippet: Any morphism with a right inverse is an epimorphism

annotations.note: IsSplitEpi.epi shows any morphism admitting a section (right inverse) is an epimorphism.

annotations.label: Section of a morphism

anchor.snippet: is called a section of f

annotations.note: SplitEpi.section_ is the section morphism satisfying section_ ≫ f = 𝟙.

annotations.label: Split epimorphism

anchor.snippet: is called a split epimorphism

annotations.note: IsSplitEpi (and the structure SplitEpi) formalize a morphism admitting a section, i.e. a split epimorphism.

annotations.label: Surjection as a binary relation

anchor.snippet: is then a binary relation between X and Y that is right-unique and both left-total and right-total

annotations.note: Mathlib does not characterize surjections as right-unique, left- and right-total binary relations.

annotations.label: Cardinality of domain dominates codomain

anchor.section: Cardinality of the domain of a surjection
anchor.snippet: The cardinality of the domain of a surjective function is greater than or equal to the cardinality of its codomain

annotations.note: Cardinal.mk_le_of_surjective gives #β ≤ #α for a surjection f : α → β.

annotations.label: Finite equal-size: surjective iff injective

anchor.section: Cardinality of the domain of a surjection
anchor.snippet: if both X and Y are finite with the same number of elements

annotations.note: injective_iff_surjective_of_equiv proves Injective f ↔ Surjective f for f between finite types of equal cardinality.

annotations.label: Composition of surjections is surjective

anchor.snippet: The composition of surjective functions is always surjective

annotations.note: Function.Surjective.comp shows the composite of two surjective functions is surjective.

annotations.label: Surjection-injection decomposition

anchor.snippet: Any function can be decomposed into a surjection and an injection

annotations.note: The pieces (rangeFactorization surjective, subtype inclusion injective, coe_comp_rangeFactorization = f) exist but there is no single packaged surjection–injection factorization lemma for plain functions.

annotations.label: Induced surjection onto range

anchor.snippet: Any function induces a surjection by restricting its codomain to its range

annotations.note: Set.rangeFactorization corestricts f to its range and rangeFactorization_surjective proves it is surjective.

annotations.label: Induced bijection on a quotient

anchor.snippet: Any surjective function induces a bijection defined on a quotient of its domain

annotations.note: Setoid.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective gives an equiv (bijection) Quotient (ker f) ≃ β for surjective f.

annotations.label: Number of surjections (Twelvefold way)

anchor.snippet: The cardinality of this set is one of the twelve aspects of Rota's Twelvefold way

annotations.note: Stirling numbers of the second kind are defined, but the formula counting surjections between finite sets is neither connected to them nor proven.
